req.title: Submit timesheet
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two independent validation axes: per-day hours [0,24] and weekly total [0,80]. I apply EP to identify valid/invalid partitions for each axis, BVA to probe the exact boundary values (0, 24, 80 and their neighbours), STC to verify the draft→submitted state transition on the happy path, DT to combine per-day and weekly-total rule interactions, and NEG to confirm 422 responses for both error pathways. Five tests are generated: (1) EP/STC positive with a typical valid week, (2) NEG for a single day exceeding 24 h, (3) BVA for daily boundary at exactly 24 h, (4) BVA for weekly boundary at exactly 80 h, (5) DT/NEG for weekly total exceeding 80 h while every individual day is within range.

tests.name: test_submit_valid_timesheet_typical_week
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: Build a payload with Mon=8, Tue=8, Wed=8, Thu=8, Fri=8, Sat=0, Sun=0 (total=40)
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with valid auth header
expected: Each day value is preserved in the response
trace: R-WEB-067: valid per-day hours (0-24) and valid weekly total (0-80)
EP: representative value from the valid equivalence class for both rules

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_single_day_exceeds_24h
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
actions: Build a payload where Wednesday=25 (invalid), all other days=8
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with valid auth header
expected: Response body references the per-day validation rule or field 'wed'
trace: R-WEB-067 error pathway: 422 when per-day hours outside 0-24
NEG: value from the invalid partition (>24) for a single day

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_single_day_exactly_24h
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
actions: Build a payload where Monday=24 (upper boundary), all other days=0
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with valid auth header
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 (24 is the inclusive upper bound, so the request is valid)
trace: BVA: test the exact upper boundary value 24 for a single day

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_weekly_total_exactly_80h
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
actions: Build a payload: Mon=24, Tue=24, Wed=24, Thu=8, Fri=0, Sat=0, Sun=0 (total=80)
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with valid auth header
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 (80 is the inclusive upper bound for weekly total)
trace: R-WEB-067: total week hours between 0 and 80 (inclusive)
BVA: test the exact upper boundary value 80 for weekly total

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80h_all_days_valid
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
actions: Build a payload: Mon=24, Tue=24, Wed=24, Thu=9, Fri=0, Sat=0, Sun=0 (total=81; each day <=24)
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with valid auth header
expected: Per-day rule passes; weekly-total rule fires; error references weekly/total limit
trace: R-WEB-067 error pathway: 422 when weekly total outside 0-80
DT: per-day rule=PASS, weekly-total rule=FAIL — isolates the second validation dimension

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
A timesheet in state='draft' already exists for the target week (created via <create_draft_fixture>)
actions: Retrieve the draft timesheet id via <create_draft_fixture>
POST valid payload (Mon-Fri=8h each, total=40) to /timesheets/submit referencing the draft id
expected: A subsequent GET on the same timesheet id returns state='submitted'
trace: STC: verifies the draft -> submitted state transition explicitly
